title: AN ACT ESTABLISHING A NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL IN BARANGAY BALAONG, MUNICIPALITY OF SAN MIGUEL, PROVINCE OF BULACAN TO BE KNOWN AS BALAONG NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

chunks.text:
H. No. 4018

[ REPUBLIC ACT NO. 9713, August 26, 2009 ]

AN ACT ESTABLISHING A NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL IN BARANGAY BALAONG, MUNICIPALITY OF SAN MIGUEL, PROVINCE OF BULACAN TO BE KNOWN AS BALAONG NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

Be it enacted by the Senate and House of Representative of the Philippines in Congress assembled:

SECTION 1.

Establishment of a National High School.

芒聙聰 There is hereby established, under the supervision of the Department of Education, a national high school in Barangay Balaong, Municipality of San Miguel, Province of Bulacan to be known as Balaong National High School.

SEC. 2.

Appropriations.

芒聙聰 The Secretary of Education shall immediately include in the Department's program the operationalization of Balaong National High School, the funding of which shall be included in the annual General Appropriations Act.

SEC. 3.

Implementation.

芒聙聰 The Secretary of Education shall immediately include in the Department's program the operationalization of Maronquillo National High School, the funding of which shall be included in the annual General Appropriations Act.

SEC. 3.

Implementation.

芒聙聰 The Secretary of Education shall issue such rules and regulations as may be necessary to carry out the purpose of this Act.

SEC. 4.

Effectivity.

芒聙聰 This Act shall take effect fifteen (15) days after its publication in the

Official Gazette

.

Approved,

(Sgd.) JUAN PONCE ENRILE

(Sgd.) PROSPERO C. NOGRALES

President of Senate

Speaker of the House of Representatives

This Act which originated in the House of Representatives was finally passed by the House of Representatives and the Senate on May 28, 2008 and May 4, 2009, respectively.

(Sgd.) EMMA LIRIO-REYES

(Sgd.) MARILYN B. BARUA-YAP

Secretary of Senate

Secretary General House of Representatives

Approved: AUG 26 2009

(Sgd.) GLORIA MACAPAGAL - ARROYO

President of the Philippines
